regions.text: Eratosthenes (276-194 B.C.) was the first to work out the earth's size. He used a deep well and the angle of the sun's light.
By using geometry, he calculated the earth was 29,000 miles round. Scientists now say it is about 25,000 miles round.
(Diagram: Sun's light hitting a well at 7.2 degrees to measure the earth's circumference from its middle point).
well: a deep hole for water. bottom: lowest part, opposite of top. Arrow points to the bottom of the glass.
